task_completion.description: Verify that the simulation was run and stop_output.xml was generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command running SUMO or a file listing showing 'stop_output.xml' in the /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of modal_performance.csv and bus_stop_analysis.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'modal_performance.csv' and 'bus_stop_analysis.csv' in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/. If their contents are visible, modal_performance should show metrics for buses and cars, and bus_stop_analysis should list stops with bus counts, dwell times, and headways.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of underserved_stops.csv containing stops with <3 visits or >1200s headway.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'underserved_stops.csv' in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/. If open in an editor or printed in the terminal, it should contain a list of specific bus stop IDs.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the new bus route XML file serving underserved stops.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'acosta_new_bus_route.rou.xml' in /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/. If open, it should contain valid SUMO XML routing syntax with at least 2 new vehicles and stops referencing the underserved list.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the modified SUMO configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'run_improved_transit.sumocfg' in /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/. It should be a valid SUMO config file that includes the new bus route file.

task_completion.description: Verify the completion of the final transit assessment report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'transit_assessment_report.txt' in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/. If open, it should contain text discussing modal split, stop utilization, identified gaps, and proposed impacts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually ran the SUMO simulation to generate the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal history showing 'sumo' or 'sumo-gui' commands being executed with the scenario configuration, rather than the agent just creating the output XML/CSVs manually.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a script (e.g., Python) or data tool to compute the metrics.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Python scripts, pandas usage, or terminal commands parsing the XML files. The agent should not just hallucinate or manually type fake numbers into the CSV files.

integrity.description: Ensure the newly created XML files are properly formatted.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that 'acosta_new_bus_route.rou.xml' and 'run_improved_transit.sumocfg' look like genuine XML files with proper tags, not just plain text descriptions of what the file should do.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The scenario 'bologna_acosta' contains exactly 35 bus stops and 157 bus vehicles. The agent must process data to find underserved stops (<3 visits or >1200s headway). The new route file must contain at least 2 new vehicles and serve at least 3 of these underserved stops. Ensure all files are saved in their exact requested directories (/home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/ for scenario files, /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ for analysis/report files).
